text: When facing an important problem, I am usually:
optionS: Methodical - I work steadily through the problem step by step
optionI: Dialogue-seeking - I discuss it with others to get input
optionC: Analytical - I thoroughly examine all facts before deciding
optionD: Action-oriented - I make decisions quickly and get started

text: In a stressful situation with deadlines, I prefer to:
optionS: Stay calm and work systematically through the tasks
optionI: Gather the team and keep the mood positive
optionC: Ensure quality does not suffer under pressure
optionD: Take control and prioritize what needs to be done first

text: If I discover an error in a project, I will typically:
optionS: Fix it quietly without creating drama
optionI: Discuss it openly with the team so we learn from it
optionC: Analyze why the error occurred so we can prevent it in the future
optionD: Handle it immediately and move on

text: When I need to learn something new, my preferred approach is:
optionS: Follow step-by-step instructions at my own pace
optionI: Learn together with others through discussion and sharing experiences
optionC: Study the material thoroughly and understand the theory first
optionD: Jump right in and learn through practice

text: My typical reaction to sudden changes in the plan is:
optionS: I prefer stability, but adapt gradually
optionI: Be curious about the new possibilities it brings
optionC: Evaluate the consequences before accepting the change
optionD: See it as an opportunity and adapt quickly

text: When a project is not going as planned, I tend to:
optionS: Remain calm and help stabilize the situation
optionI: Keep the team morale up and find the positive in the situation
optionC: Identify exactly what went wrong and how we fix it
optionD: Make quick decisions to get things back on track

text: In meetings, I most often contribute by:
optionS: Listening attentively and supporting others' ideas
optionI: Contributing energy and getting others engaged
optionC: Asking critical questions and pointing out potential challenges
optionD: Driving the agenda forward and ensuring we reach our goals

text: When I need to give feedback to a colleague, I prefer to:
optionS: Be patient and diplomatic in my approach
optionI: Start positive and create a good dialogue about improvements
optionC: Give concrete, fact-based feedback with specific examples
optionD: Be direct and results-oriented in my communication

text: My preferred work environment is one where:
optionS: There is stability, predictability, and a supportive team
optionI: There is good chemistry, laughter, and open communication
optionC: There are high standards and time to do things properly
optionD: There are clear goals and I have freedom to reach them my way

text: In team discussions, I am typically the one who:
optionS: Ensures all voices are heard
optionI: Promotes brainstorming and gets everyone to participate
optionC: Asks questions about quality and consistency
optionD: Keeps focus on the bottom line and next steps

text: When presenting an idea, I focus most on:
optionS: Showing how it benefits everyone and creates harmony
optionI: Enthusing others and building support
optionC: Documenting research and supporting with data
optionD: Demonstrating results and potential impact

text: My communication style is best described as:
optionS: Patient, friendly, and supportive
optionI: Enthusiastic, outgoing, and engaging
optionC: Precise, well-considered, and well-argued
optionD: Direct, clear, and efficient

text: When starting a new project, I will naturally:
optionS: Build a stable process that ensures steady progress
optionI: Form a team and kick off with an inspiring vision
optionC: Research thoroughly and plan in detail before starting
optionD: Identify key goals and get started right away

text: My relationship with rules and procedures is:
optionS: I follow them loyally - they create structure and security
optionI: I see them as guidelines, but focus on the people
optionC: They are important, and I ensure they are followed correctly
optionD: I follow them when they make sense, but challenge them if necessary

text: Under time pressure, my priority is typically:
optionS: To keep processes running and avoid chaos
optionI: To keep the team motivated and collaborative
optionC: To ensure we don't compromise on quality
optionD: To reach the goals - if necessary by taking shortcuts

text: When working alone on a task, I am best at:
optionS: Working steadily and reliably through the task
optionI: Generating creative ideas and innovative solutions
optionC: Producing error-free, well-researched work
optionD: Working quickly and getting things done

text: My natural work rhythm can be described as:
optionS: Steady and consistent with predictable routines
optionI: Varied and energetic with room for spontaneity
optionC: Focused and thorough with time for details
optionD: Intense and fast-paced with focus on results

text: When I am most productive, I work best:
optionS: In a quiet, stable environment without constant interruptions
optionI: In collaboration with others where we can brainstorm
optionC: With the opportunity for deep concentration and analysis
optionD: With clear deadlines and challenges to solve

text: What motivates me most in my work is:
optionS: Being a reliable part of a team that supports each other
optionI: Working with people and making a positive difference
optionC: Delivering expertise and quality work
optionD: Achieving concrete results and reaching ambitious goals

text: When I reflect on a good workday, it's because I have:
optionS: Helped the team and contributed to a harmonious environment
optionI: Had great interactions and inspired others
optionC: Solved complex problems or produced error-free work
optionD: Reached my goals and overcome challenges

text: My colleagues would probably describe me as:
optionS: Loyal, patient, and supportive
optionI: Positive, social, and inspiring
optionC: Thorough, precise, and quality-conscious
optionD: Goal-oriented, decisive, and results-driven

text: What is most important to me in a leadership role is to:
optionS: Be a stable and reliable leader people can count on
optionI: Create a positive environment where people thrive
optionC: Maintain high standards and delivery of quality
optionD: Drive performance and achieve ambitious results

text: I feel most valued when others recognize my:
optionS: Loyalty, reliability, and supportive nature
optionI: Enthusiasm, creativity, and ability to engage others
optionC: Expertise, precision, and thoroughness
optionD: Decisiveness, results, and leadership

text: My definition of success at work is primarily:
optionS: Being a faithful team player who contributes to stability
optionI: Creating positive relationships and inspiring others
optionC: Delivering error-free, well-researched work of high quality

text: Generally speaking, I tend to:
optionS: I work equally well with action as with discussion
optionI: I am more interested in discussion than in action
optionC: I analyze the situation before action or discussion
optionD: I am more interested in action than in discussion

text: When something goes wrong, my first reaction is:
optionS: I stay calm and help calm the situation
optionI: I gather people to handle it together
optionC: I analyze what went wrong to prevent it from happening again
optionD: I take immediate action to fix it

text: Others would say I am most known for being:
optionS: Never disagreeing with anyone about anything
optionI: Always happy and never stressed
optionC: Reliable and never making mistakes
optionD: Always in the driver's seat and making all decisions alone
